differential_diagnoses: Primary Angiitis of the Central Nervous System (PACNS)
Leptomeningeal Metastasis (e.g., primary CNS lymphoma or occult systemic malignancy)
Mitochondrial Encephalomyopathy, Lactic Acidosis, and Stroke-like episodes (MELAS)
next_steps: Brain and meningeal biopsy (gold standard for inflammatory/neoplastic diagnosis)
Contrast-enhanced MRI of the brain/spine (focus on leptomeningeal enhancement patterns)
Repeat cerebrospinal fluid analysis including cytology, flow cytometry, and paraneoplastic/autoimmune panels
Whole-body PET/CT to screen for occult malignancy or systemic inflammatory disease
Consider empiric corticosteroid trial if biopsy is delayed, following CSF analysis
Video EEG monitoring to characterize the tremulous episodes and visual disturbances

differential_diagnoses: Primary Central Nervous System Lymphoma (Leptomeningeal variant)
Meningeal Metastasis from occult solid tumor (e.g., melanoma)
next_steps: Brain and leptomeningeal biopsy targeting areas of nodular enhancement/hyperintensity
Repeat high-volume cerebrospinal fluid analysis with flow cytometry, cytology, and paraneoplastic antibody panels
MRI of the entire neuroaxis with gadolinium contrast to evaluate for occult spinal leptomeningeal involvement
Funduscopic examination and retinal imaging to rule out vasculitis or infiltrative processes
Whole-body PET/CT imaging to screen for systemic malignancy or evidence of IgG4-related systemic involvement
Video EEG monitoring to definitively rule out subclinical seizure activity related to the cortical involvement
